prompt: I'm building a marketing page with Next.js 15 (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind. I have a Features section with an array of 6 feature cards (each is { id, icon, title, description }). I want each card to fade up and in as the section scrolls into view, staggered one after the other — but only the first time it enters (no re-animating if the user scrolls up and back down). I also want the whole thing to respect prefers-reduced-motion. Give me the full Features.tsx component file — I'll pass the features array in as a prop. Use Motion for React (not framer-motion).
assertions: Uses variants (not individual delay props on each item)
Uses stagger() from motion/react in delayChildren
Uses whileInView with viewport={{ once: true }}
Handles prefers-reduced-motion via useReducedMotion() or MotionConfig
Writes valid TSX with proper typing for the features prop
Does not use any Motion+ components (Cursor, AnimateNumber, Ticker)
Does not reference Motion Studio MCP in generated code or comments
